Workshop Roadmap

This workshop covers the principles of conducting a rigorous systematic review, in line with PRISMA guidance, and reinforces professional effectiveness, academic citizenship and research ethics.

  1. Work through all 6 Training Modules.
  2. Take the Assessment Quiz — you need 60% or higher to pass.
  3. Download / print your personalised Certificate of Completion.

Official PRISMA 2020 Resources
PRISMA Checklist → OSF Register (STEM) → PROSPERO (Clinical) →
🔵 Protocol Registration Guide
Where to register your systematic review protocol — OSF for STEM / Materials Science, PROSPERO for clinical topics
🔑 Key Rule — Register BEFORE You Search
Protocol registration should happen before you start searching databases. Registering only after seeing your results risks outcome-reporting bias — a serious methodological flaw. PRISMA 2020 Item 26 requires you to state your registration platform and ID number in your manuscript.

🔬 Which Register for Your Research Domain?

Research DomainRecommended RegisterReason

✍ Registration Statement Templates (Copy & Paste)

OSF Statement (for STEM / Materials Science):
This systematic review protocol was prospectively registered on the Open Science Framework (OSF) Registries on [DATE] (DOI: https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/[YOUR-ID]). OSF was selected as the registration platform as this review addresses [your field] research, for which OSF Registries is a recommended cross-disciplinary systematic review registration platform.
PROSPERO Statement (for health-related topics):
This systematic review was prospectively registered on PROSPERO on [DATE] with registration number CRD20[XXXXXXXX], available at: https://www.crd.york.ac.uk/prospero/display_record.php?ID=CRD20[XXXXXXXX]

📖 Why OSF Registration Works for Non-Clinical Research

Guidance on systematic review registration platforms generally treats OSF Registries as a valid, discipline-neutral option alongside PROSPERO, INPLASY, the Research Registry and protocols.io — and notes it is open to any study type, which makes it well suited to materials science, chemistry, engineering and other non-clinical fields.

A growing number of published, peer-reviewed non-clinical systematic reviews now cite an OSF registration DOI in their methods sections, confirming that journals in materials science and engineering accept OSF as a legitimate prospective registration platform.

Training Modules
Open each module and read through the content. Modules are marked complete automatically as you view them.
Assessment Quiz
Answer all questions. You need a score of 60% or higher to receive your certificate. You can retake the quiz any time.
🤖 AI Assist Settings
Optional — bring your own Mistral or DeepSeek API key to unlock AI-powered feedback, drafting and review. The app works fully without one.

How this works

Everything in this app — training, quiz, scoring, certificate — already works fully offline using built-in rule-based logic. Adding your own API key is entirely optional and only adds a richer, AI-generated layer of feedback on top.

  • Your key is stored only in this browser (localStorage) and is never sent anywhere except directly to the provider you choose.
  • It is never saved to a server, file, or shared with anyone else using this app.
  • Remove it any time with the button below — your offline scoring keeps working either way.
  • AI calls require an internet connection at the moment you use them; offline scoring always remains available as a fallback.
  • Reading text out of PDF and Word files happens entirely offline in your browser (no internet, no upload to any server) — only the extracted text is sent to your chosen AI provider when you click an AI button.

1. Choose a Provider

🌬️
Mistral AI
Fast, low-cost, EU-hosted. Good for drafting, summaries and citation help.
Get a Mistral API key →
🐳
DeepSeek
Strong reasoning per dollar. Good for in-depth PRISMA critique & scoring.
Get a DeepSeek API key →

2. Where AI Assist Shows Up

✍️ Review Editor — "🤖 AI Feedback" gives qualitative PRISMA critique beyond the rule-based score.
🎯 PICO & Search Builder — "🤖 Draft with AI" suggests PICO elements and a search string from your topic.
📚 Article Library — "🤖 Summarize" extracts text from PDF/DOCX/TXT offline, then suggests a short summary and domain tag.

🔒 Security — API Key Management

Your API keys are sensitive credentials. They are stored in your browser's localStorage and sent only to Mistral/Gemini. They are never sent to any other server, but:
• Anyone with access to this browser can extract them via DevTools.
• Always remove keys when using a shared or public computer.
• Keys are automatically wiped when you log out of this app.
✨ AI Review
Use Mistral or Gemini to proofread your manuscript, check its novelty, and get recommendations — requires a completed manuscript in the Review Editor.

🎓 My Certificate
Your personalised UNISA Certificate of Participation — issued upon completing the requirements below.
About this Workshop

Domain: A1 Knowledge Base

Information and Data Literacy Management · Academic Literacy · Research Methods — Level 4.

This stand-alone training application was designed for offline and online workshop delivery on Professional effectiveness, academic citizenship and ethics in the context of systematic review research methodology, presented under the UNISA Researcher Development Academy / Postdoctoral Research Fellowship programme. The entire app — training content, quiz and certificate generation — runs locally in your browser with no internet connection required after the first load.
